edits.text:
Notes:
    - Body text is intentionally NOT heavily cleaned here. Ads / boilerplate / promo
      sentences may remain; detailed sentence cleaning is a separate later step.
    - `outlet_block` is descriptive metadata only, not a ground-truth ideology label
      and not a target variable.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import os
import time
from pathlib import Path

import feedparser
import requests
import trafilatura
from dotenv import load_dotenv

from utils import PROCESSED, RAW_HTML, write_jsonl

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / ".env")

# --- Outlet plan -------------------------------------------------------------
# Each block maps a short source name -> list of RSS feed URLs for that outlet.
# `outlet_block` is exploratory metadata only (descriptive, never causal, never a
# ground-truth ideology label). Outlets are widely categorised by independent media
# bias raters; we use that only to balance the two collection buckets.
RSS_FEEDS: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {
    "left": {
        "theguardian": [
            "https://www.theguardian.com/world/rss",
            "https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/rss",
            "https://www.theguardian.com/politics/rss",
        ],
        "npr": [
            "https://feeds.npr.org/1001/rss.xml",  # News
            "https://feeds.npr.org/1014/rss.xml",  # Politics
            "https://feeds.npr.org/1003/rss.xml",  # National
        ],
        "cnn": [
            "http://rss.cnn.com/rss/cnn_topstories.rss",
            "http://rss.cnn.com/rss/cnn_us.rss",
            "http://rss.cnn.com/rss/cnn_allpolitics.rss",
        ],
    },
    "right": {
        "foxnews": [
            "https://moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/latest.xml",
            "https://moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/politics.xml",
            "https://moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/us.xml",
        ],
        "nypost": [
            "https://nypost.com/feed/",
            "https://nypost.com/news/feed/",
            "https://nypost.com/us-news/feed/",
        ],
        "washingtonexaminer": [
            "https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/feed",
            "https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/section/news/feed",
            "https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/section/politics/feed",
        ],
    },
}

TARGET_PER_BLOCK = 150

USER_AGENT = os.getenv(
    "USER_AGENT",
    "media-bias-pipeline/0.1 (research; contact: your-email@example.com)",
)
CRAWL_DELAY_SEC = float(os.getenv("CRAWL_DELAY_SEC", "1.0"))
MIN_BODY_CHARS = 400  # skip stubs / non-article pages


def sha1(text: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha1(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


def collect_feed_urls(feeds: list[str], session: requests.Session) -> list[str]:
    """Return de-duplicated article URLs from a list of RSS feed URLs (order preserved)."""
    seen: set[str] = set()
    urls: list[str] = []
    for feed_url in feeds:
        try:
            resp = session.get(feed_url, timeout=30)
            parsed = feedparser.parse(resp.content)
        except Exception as exc:  # network / parse error — skip this feed
            print(f"    ! feed error {feed_url}: {exc}")
            continue
        n_before = len(urls)
        for entry in parsed.entries:
            link = entry.get("link")
            if not link:
                continue
            link = link.split("#")[0].strip()
            if link in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(link)
            urls.append(link)
        print(f"    {feed_url} -> {len(urls) - n_before} new urls")
        time.sleep(CRAWL_DELAY_SEC)
    return urls


def fetch_html(url: str, session: requests.Session) -> str | None:
    """Fetch and cache raw HTML for a URL. Returns HTML text or None on failure."""
    cache_path = RAW_HTML / f"{sha1(url)}.html"
    if cache_path.exists():
        return cache_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
    try:
        resp = session.get(url, timeout=30)
        resp.raise_for_status()
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"    ! fetch error {url}: {exc}")
        return None
    html = resp.text
    cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cache_path.write_text(html, encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
    time.sleep(CRAWL_DELAY_SEC)
    return html


def extract_article(html: str, url: str, source: str, block: str) -> dict | None:
    """Extract a structured article dict from raw HTML using trafilatura."""
    extracted = trafilatura.extract(
        html,
        url=url,
        output_format="json",
        with_metadata=True,
        favor_recall=True,
        include_comments=False,
        include_tables=False,
    )
    if not extracted:
        return None
    meta = json.loads(extracted)
    body = (meta.get("text") or "").strip()
    if len(body) < MIN_BODY_CHARS:
        return None

    # Lead: prefer the article description, else first non-empty paragraph of body.
    lead = (meta.get("description") or "").strip()
    if not lead:
        for para in body.split("\n"):
            para = para.strip()
            if para:
                lead = para
                break

    return {
        "article_id": sha1(url),
        "url": url,
        "source": source,
        "outlet_block": block,
        "title": (meta.get("title") or "").strip(),
        "lead": lead,
        "body": body,
        "image_url": meta.get("image") or None,
        "image_path": None,
        "published_at": meta.get("date") or None,
        "language": "en",
    }


def crawl_block(block: str, sources: dict[str, list[str]], target: int,
                session: requests.Session) -> list[dict]:
    """Collect up to `target` articles for one outlet block, balanced across sources."""
    print(f"\n=== Block: {block} (target {target}) ===")

    # 1) Gather candidate URLs per source.
    candidates: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for source, feeds in sources.items():
        print(f"  [{source}] reading feeds...")
        candidates[source] = collect_feed_urls(feeds, session)
        print(f"  [{source}] {len(candidates[source])} candidate urls")

    # 2) Round-robin across sources so no single outlet dominates the block.
    articles: list[dict] = []
    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
    cursors = {s: 0 for s in candidates}
    active = [s for s in candidates if candidates[s]]
    while len(articles) < target and active:
        for source in list(active):
            if len(articles) >= target:
                break
            urls = candidates[source]
            i = cursors[source]
            if i >= len(urls):
                active.remove(source)
                continue
            cursors[source] += 1
            url = urls[i]
            aid = sha1(url)
            if aid in seen_ids:
                continue
            html = fetch_html(url, session)
            if not html:
                continue
            art = extract_article(html, url, source, block)
            if not art:
                continue
            seen_ids.add(aid)
            articles.append(art)
            if len(articles) % 10 == 0:
                print(f"  ...{len(articles)}/{target} articles")
    print(f"  => collected {len(articles)} articles for block '{block}'")
    return articles


def main() -> None:
    session = requests.Session()
    session.headers.update({"User-Agent": USER_AGENT})

    all_articles: list[dict] = []
    for block, sources in RSS_FEEDS.items():
        all_articles.extend(crawl_block(block, sources, TARGET_PER_BLOCK, session))

    out_path = PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl"
    n = write_jsonl(out_path, all_articles)

    # Summary
    by_block: dict[str, int] = {}
    by_source: dict[str, int] = {}
    for a in all_articles:
        by_block[a["outlet_block"]] = by_block.get(a["outlet_block"], 0) + 1
        by_source[a["source"]] = by_source.get(a["source"], 0) + 1
    print(f"\nWrote {n} articles -> {out_path}")
    print(f"By block:  {by_block}")
    print(f"By source: {by_source}

toolCalls.arguments:
{"filePath": "c:\Users\z0056bwr\Documents\phd\task first\new task\media-bias-pipeline\src\crawl.py", "content": """"Step: crawl — collect article URLs from RSS feeds, fetch HTML, and extract structured articles.

Targets:
    - 300 articles total (150 left-block + 150 right-block).
    - Outlet block is exploratory metadata only, NOT a ground-truth ideology label.

Outputs:
    - data/raw/html/<sha1(url)>.html
    - data/processed/articles.jsonl  with schema:
        {
          "article_id":   str,              # sha1(url)
          "url":          str,
          "source":       str,              # outlet domain or short name
          "outlet_block": "left" | "right", # metadata only
          "title":        str,
          "lead":         str,              # first paragraph(s)
          "body":         str,              # cleaned full text
          "image_url":    str | None,       # main image URL (metadata only; never sent to VLM)
          "image_path":   str | None,       # local cached path once downloaded
          "published_at": str | None,       # ISO 8601 if available
          "language":     "en"
        }

Notes:
    - Body text is intentionally NOT heavily cleaned here. Ads / boilerplate / promo
      sentences may remain; detailed sentence cleaning is a separate later step.
    - `outlet_block` is descriptive metadata only, not a ground-truth ideology label
      and not a target variable.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import os
import time
from pathlib import Path

import feedparser
import requests
import trafilatura
from dotenv import load_dotenv

from utils import PROCESSED, RAW_HTML, write_jsonl

load_dotenv(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / ".env")

# --- Outlet plan -------------------------------------------------------------
# Each block maps a short source name -> list of RSS feed URLs for that outlet.
# `outlet_block` is exploratory metadata only (descriptive, never causal, never a
# ground-truth ideology label). Outlets are widely categorised by independent media
# bias raters; we use that only to balance the two collection buckets.
RSS_FEEDS: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {
    "left": {
        "theguardian": [
            "https://www.theguardian.com/world/rss",
            "https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/rss",
            "https://www.theguardian.com/politics/rss",
        ],
        "npr": [
            "https://feeds.npr.org/1001/rss.xml",  # News
            "https://feeds.npr.org/1014/rss.xml",  # Politics
            "https://feeds.npr.org/1003/rss.xml",  # National
        ],
        "cnn": [
            "http://rss.cnn.com/rss/cnn_topstories.rss",
            "http://rss.cnn.com/rss/cnn_us.rss",
            "http://rss.cnn.com/rss/cnn_allpolitics.rss",
        ],
    },
    "right": {
        "foxnews": [
            "https://moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/latest.xml",
            "https://moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/politics.xml",
            "https://moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/us.xml",
        ],
        "nypost": [
            "https://nypost.com/feed/",
            "https://nypost.com/news/feed/",
            "https://nypost.com/us-news/feed/",
        ],
        "washingtonexaminer": [
            "https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/feed",
            "https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/section/news/feed",
            "https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/section/politics/feed",
        ],
    },
}

TARGET_PER_BLOCK = 150

USER_AGENT = os.getenv(
    "USER_AGENT",
    "media-bias-pipeline/0.1 (research; contact: your-email@example.com)",
)
CRAWL_DELAY_SEC = float(os.getenv("CRAWL_DELAY_SEC", "1.0"))
MIN_BODY_CHARS = 400  # skip stubs / non-article pages


def sha1(text: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha1(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


def collect_feed_urls(feeds: list[str], session: requests.Session) -> list[str]:
    """Return de-duplicated article URLs from a list of RSS feed URLs (order preserved)."""
    seen: set[str] = set()
    urls: list[str] = []
    for feed_url in feeds:
        try:
            resp = session.get(feed_url, timeout=30)
            parsed = feedparser.parse(resp.content)
        except Exception as exc:  # network / parse error — skip this feed
            print(f"    ! feed error {feed_url}: {exc}")
            continue
        n_before = len(urls)
        for entry in parsed.entries:
            link = entry.get("link")
            if not link:
                continue
            link = link.split("#")[0].strip()
            if link in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(link)
            urls.append(link)
        print(f"    {feed_url} -> {len(urls) - n_before} new urls")
        time.sleep(CRAWL_DELAY_SEC)
    return urls


def fetch_html(url: str, session: requests.Session) -> str | None:
    """Fetch and cache raw HTML for a URL. Returns HTML text or None on failure."""
    cache_path = RAW_HTML / f"{sha1(url)}.html"
    if cache_path.exists():
        return cache_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
    try:
        resp = session.get(url, timeout=30)
        resp.raise_for_status()
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"    ! fetch error {url}: {exc}")
        return None
    html = resp.text
    cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cache_path.write_text(html, encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
    time.sleep(CRAWL_DELAY_SEC)
    return html


def extract_article(html: str, url: str, source: str, block: str) -> dict | None:
    """Extract a structured article dict from raw HTML using trafilatura."""
    extracted = trafilatura.extract(
        html,
        url=url,
        output_format="json",
        with_metadata=True,
        favor_recall=True,
        include_comments=False,
        include_tables=False,
    )
    if not extracted:
        return None
    meta = json.loads(extracted)
    body = (meta.get("text") or "").strip()
    if len(body) < MIN_BODY_CHARS:
        return None

    # Lead: prefer the article description, else first non-empty paragraph of body.
    lead = (meta.get("description") or "").strip()
    if not lead:
        for para in body.split("\n"):
            para = para.strip()
            if para:
                lead = para
                break

    return {
        "article_id": sha1(url),
        "url": url,
        "source": source,
        "outlet_block": block,
        "title": (meta.get("title") or "").strip(),
        "lead": lead,
        "body": body,
        "image_url": meta.get("image") or None,
        "image_path": None,
        "published_at": meta.get("date") or None,
        "language": "en",
    }


def crawl_block(block: str, sources: dict[str, list[str]], target: int,
                session: requests.Session) -> list[dict]:
    """Collect up to `target` articles for one outlet block, balanced across sources."""
    print(f"\n=== Block: {block} (target {target}) ===")

    # 1) Gather candidate URLs per source.
    candidates: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for source, feeds in sources.items():
        print(f"  [{source}] reading feeds...")
        candidates[source] = collect_feed_urls(feeds, session)
        print(f"  [{source}] {len(candidates[source])} candidate urls")

    # 2) Round-robin across sources so no single outlet dominates the block.
    articles: list[dict] = []
    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
    cursors = {s: 0 for s in candidates}
    active = [s for s in candidates if candidates[s]]
    while len(articles) < target and active:
        for source in list(active):
            if len(articles) >= target:
                break
            urls = candidates[source]
            i = cursors[source]
            if i >= len(urls):
                active.remove(source)
                continue
            cursors[source] += 1
            url = urls[i]
            aid = sha1(url)
            if aid in seen_ids:
                continue
            html = fetch_html(url, session)
            if not html:
                continue
            art = extract_article(html, url, source, block)
            if not art:
                continue
            seen_ids.add(aid)
            articles.append(art)
            if len(articles) % 10 == 0:
                print(f"  ...{len(articles)}/{target} articles")
    print(f"  => collected {len(articles)} articles for block '{block}'")
    return articles


def main() -> None:
    session = requests.Session()
    session.headers.update({"User-Agent": USER_AGENT})

    all_articles: list[dict] = []
    for block, sources in RSS_FEEDS.items():
        all_articles.extend(crawl_block(block, sources, TARGET_PER_BLOCK, session))

    out_path = PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl"
    n = write_jsonl(out_path, all_articles)

    # Summary
    by_block: dict[str, int] = {}
    by_source: dict[str, int] = {}
    for a in all_articles:
        by_block[a["outlet_block"]] = by_block.get(a["outlet_block"], 0) + 1
        by_source[a["source"]] = by_source.get(a["source"], 0) + 1
    print(f"\nWrote {n} articles -> {out_path}")
    print(f"By block:  {by_block}")
    print(f"By source: {by_source}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"}

children.text: These rules apply to **every** AI-assistant action in this repository.

For **every** discrete step the assistant performs (scaffolding, writing a script, running an experiment, fixing a bug, making a design decision, etc.), append a new entry to [ai_usage/step_logs.md](ai_usage/step_logs.md).
- **Append only.** Never edit or delete prior entries retroactively, even to fix typos or reframe a decision. If a previous step turned out wrong, add a new entry that supersedes it.
- **One entry per step.** Do not batch multiple unrelated steps into one entry.
- **Write the entry in the same turn** that produced the step — not later, not "I'll log this at the end."
- **Goal:** what we set out to do
- **Action:** what the assistant did (scripts touched, commands run, decisions made)
- **Prompt / model:** which model + a one-line description of the prompt or instruction
- **Outcome:** result, files produced, anything that broke
Implement only what the current step asks for. Defer extras to a follow-up step (and log them).
New dependencies → `requirements.txt`. New env vars → `.env.example`. New pipeline outputs → documented in `README.md`.
- **No `config.yaml`.** Runtime settings come from `.env`; small fixed constants live in `src/` modules.
- `.env` is **never** committed; `.env.example` is. Both are listed in `.gitignore` rules accordingly.
- All prompts live under `prompts/`. Do not inline prompts inside Python files.
- **Do not call LLM annotations "gold labels"** — they are silver / reference annotations.
- **Do not claim outlet-block effects are causal**, and **do not equate subjectivity with bias**, in code comments, docs, or report text.
## Rule 5 — External model facts must be verified

Before writing client code against an external model (OpenAI, HF, etc.), confirm the model ID, supported endpoints, modalities, context window, and output limits against the **official docs**, and record the verification in `ai_usage/step_logs.md`.
Never send image **URLs** to the VLM. Always download, optionally resize/compress, base64-encode, and send the image inline. Keep `image_url` as dataset metadata only — never inside the prompt.
Small end-to-end pipeline that produces a **fine-tuning-ready dataset for sentence-level media bias detection** from English news articles. Built as a PhD technical-exam deliverable (~4–6h scope).
**Sentence-level subjective / opinionated framing bias** in English news articles.

Subjectivity is treated as a *proxy signal* for opinionated framing — not as a synonym for "media bias". The dataset is designed so a downstream classifier can be fine-tuned on sentence-level subjective-vs-objective framing, with article-level metadata (outlet, outlet block, main image) available for richer analysis.
- **150** from a left-leaning outlet block, **150** from a right-leaning outlet block.
- Concrete outlet list is chosen and documented in `src/crawl.py` (and logged in `ai_usage/step_logs.md`).
- **`outlet_block ∈ {left, right}` is exploratory metadata only**, not a ground-truth ideology label and not the target variable. Block-level differences are descriptive, never causal.
| VLM input | article main image + title + lead paragraph |
| Metadata (not features) | source, outlet_block, title, url, image_url |

> Sentence cleaning is intentionally minimal at the split stage. **Detailed sentence cleaning (boilerplate stripping, quote handling, length filtering, deduplication) is handled as a separate step** before LLM annotation, and is documented in `src/sentence_split.py` plus the corresponding step log.
- Outputs **OBJ / SUBJ** at the sentence level.
- Used as a **proxy for subjective / opinionated framing**, not as a complete media-bias detector. Expected disagreements with the LLM target task are documented in the report.
- **OpenAI `gpt-5.4-mini`** (verified against the official OpenAI docs on 2026-06-01):
- Context window: **400,000 tokens**, max output: **128,000 tokens**.
- Modalities: text + image input, text output (vision supported).
- Endpoints: `v1/chat/completions` and `v1/responses`; structured outputs + function calling supported.
- Sentence-level annotation comfortably fits the context window. We still keep prompts **compact** and **omit unnecessary article metadata** from the LLM input.
- **Prompt design for sentence annotation is intentionally deferred to a later step.** The file `prompts/llm_sentence_annotation.txt` is a placeholder until then.
- Same model family (`gpt-5.4-mini`) used in vision mode for the article main image + title + lead.
- **Images are NOT sent as URLs.** We download → resize/compress → base64-encode and pass as inline image input. This avoids leaking outlet/source identity through URLs and reduces shortcut bias.
- `image_url` stays as internal dataset metadata, never inside the VLM prompt.
├── src/                            # crawl, split, proxy_label, annotate_llm, annotate_vlm, analyze, utils
1. **Dataset overview** — article count, sentence count, outlet/block distribution, image availability.
2. **Proxy distribution** — OBJ/SUBJ counts overall and per outlet block.
3. **LLM distribution** — objective / subjective_framing overall and per outlet block.
4. **Agreement analysis** — confusion matrix, precision, recall, F1, Cohen's κ, using **LLM labels as silver / reference annotations** (not gold).
5. **Disagreement analysis** — proxy false positives and false negatives vs. the LLM reference.
6. **Sentence length** — short / medium / long buckets vs. subjectivity rate.
7. **VLM analysis** — visual framing label distribution and selected image/text examples.
- **No `config.yaml`.** All runtime settings come from `.env` (and small constants in `src/`).
- Every critical AI-assisted decision is logged forward-only as a new entry in [ai_usage/step_logs.md](ai_usage/step_logs.md).
## Claims we are careful **not** to make
- LLM labels are **silver / reference annotations**, not gold.
- **Subjectivity ≠ bias**; it is a proxy signal for one facet of opinionated framing.
- We do **not** claim classifier-grade accuracy from this small dataset.
- Chosen sub-task & justification — see top of this file.
- Decisions, shortcuts, risks — populated step-by-step in `ai_usage/step_logs.md` and summarized here.
- Proxy classifier fit & expected disagreement — TBD after Step: proxy_label.

summaries.text:
1. Conversation Overview:
- Primary Objectives: User requested (in Turkish): "Projeyi oku ve anla. RSS sistemine başlayalım. RSS sistemi kurulmuş mu checkle, sistemi anla, ardından çekimlere başla. hedefimiz sol ve sağ bloğu dolduracak şekilde istediğimiz formatlarda 300 article'ı çekmek. çekimi gerçekleştir ve her outlet'ten 5'er örneğe bakarak çekim istediğimiz gibi gerçekleşmiş mi kontrol et. bu adımda önemli olan çıktı çekimi tamamlamak istemediğimiz reklam sentence vb içeriklerin text'te olması önemli değil onları sonradan temizleyeceğiz." Translation: Read/understand the project, check if RSS system is set up, understand it, then crawl 300 articles (150 left + 150 right blocks) in the desired format, then verify by examining 5 samples per outlet. Ad/boilerplate sentences in text are fine for now (cleaned later).
- Session Context: Explored project → confirmed RSS NOT set up → set up Python env/deps → implemented crawl.py → ran crawl (NPR timeout issue) → fixed with browser UA → completed run (left=150, right=126, total=276) → expanded right feeds to reach 150 → currently re-running.
- User Intent Evolution: Consistent throughout. Communication in Turkish expected.

2. Technical Foundation:
- Python 3.12.9 (system install): interpreter path `C:/Users/z0056bwr/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe` — MUST use this full path in terminal commands (not `python`).
- feedparser 6.0.12: RSS parsing. trafilatura 2.0.0: article extraction (JSON output with metadata). beautifulsoup4, requests, python-dotenv, lxml, tqdm available.
- Workspace root: `c:\Users\z0056bwr\Documents\phd\task first\new task\media-bias-pipeline`
- Round-robin balancing pattern across sources within each block to prevent single-outlet dominance.
- Project rules (CLAUDE.md): forward-only append logging to ai_usage/step_logs.md (one entry per step, written same turn), no config.yaml, settings from .env (.env not committed, .env.example is), prompts under prompts/, outlet_block is exploratory/descriptive metadata ONLY (never causal, never ground-truth ideology label), LLM labels are "silver/reference" not "gold".

3. Codebase Status:
- src/crawl.py (FULLY IMPLEMENTED, was a stub):
  - Purpose: Collect article URLs from RSS feeds, fetch+cache HTML, extract structured articles to data/processed/articles.jsonl
  - Current State: Complete and working. Imports: hashlib, json, os, time, pathlib, feedparser, requests, trafilatura, dotenv, and `from utils import PROCESSED, RAW_HTML, write_jsonl`.
  - RSS_FEEDS structure (nested dict block→source→list[feed_urls]):
    - left: theguardian (world/us-news/politics rss), npr (feeds.npr.org 1001/1014/1003), cnn (rss.cnn.com topstories/us/allpolitics)
    - right: foxnews (moxie.foxnews.com latest/politics/us/world/opinion), nypost (feed/news/us-news/politics), washingtonexaminer (feed/news/politics), washingtontimes (rss/headlines/news/ + politics), dailycaller (feed)
  - Constants: TARGET_PER_BLOCK=150, _DEFAULT_UA (browser Chrome UA string), USER_AGENT=os.getenv("USER_AGENT") or _DEFAULT_UA, CRAWL_DELAY_SEC=1.0, REQUEST_TIMEOUT=20, MIN_BODY_CHARS=400
  - Article schema: {article_id (sha1 of url), url, source, outlet_block, title, lead (description or first paragraph), body (trafilatura text), image_url, image_path (None), published_at (date), language ("en")}
  - Key functions: sha1(), collect_feed_urls() (dedup URLs, 1s delay per feed), fetch_html() (cache to RAW_HTML/<sha1>.html, 1 retry, NOTE: failures NOT cached so re-runs re-attempt them), extract_article() (trafilatura JSON, skip if body<400 chars), crawl_block() (round-robin), main()
- requirements.txt: Added `feedparser` line after `requests` in Core section.
- src/utils.py (unchanged): ROOT=parents[1], DATA, RAW_HTML=data/raw/html, PROCESSED=data/processed, ANALYSIS; read_jsonl(), write_jsonl(path, rows) returns count.
- data/processed/articles.jsonl: Written with 276 articles from second run; being overwritten by current third run.

4. Problem Resolution:
- Issue: NPR article pages (www.npr.org) timed out at 30s each with default research UA, massively slowing crawl. Solution: Added browser-like Chrome User-Agent as default; verified NPR returns 200 in 0.7s with it.
- Issue: install_python_packages installed to wrong interpreter initially (ModuleNotFoundError). Solution: Ran `python.exe -m pip install` directly with full interpreter path.
- Issue: Right block only reached 126/150 (feeds exhausted: foxnews 66 + nypost 32 + washingtonexaminer 28 = 126 candidates). Solution: Added Fox world/opinion feeds, NYPost politics feed, and 2 new sources (washingtontimes ~40, dailycaller ~20) — all validated working.
- Known Issue: CNN topstories RSS feed contains many stale 2023 "cnn-underscored" shopping links that return 403 Forbidden. These are skipped but NOT cached (failures aren't cached), so each re-run re-attempts ~30 junk URLs with retries+delays, slowing left block re-processing. This is cosmetic/slow but not blocking — left still reaches 150.

5. Progress Tracking:

1. Conversation Overview:
- Primary Objectives: User requested (in Turkish): "Projeyi oku ve anla. RSS sistemine başlayalım. RSS sistemi kurulmuş mu checkle, sistemi anla, ardından çekimlere başla. hedefimiz sol ve sağ bloğu dolduracak şekilde istediğimiz formatlarda 300 article'ı çekmek. çekimi gerçekleştir ve her outlet'ten 5'er örneğe bakarak çekim istediğimiz gibi gerçekleşmiş mi kontrol et. bu adımda önemli olan çıktı çekimi tamamlamak istemediğimiz reklam sentence vb içeriklerin text'te olması önemli değil onları sonradan temizleyeceğiz." Translation: Read/understand the project, check if RSS system is set up, understand it, then crawl 300 articles (150 left + 150 right blocks) in the desired format, then verify by examining 5 samples per outlet. Ad/boilerplate sentences in text are fine for now (cleaned later).
- Session Context: Explored project → confirmed RSS NOT set up → set up Python env/deps → implemented crawl.py → ran crawl (NPR timeout issue) → fixed with browser UA → completed run (left=150, right=126, total=276) → expanded right feeds to reach 150 → currently re-running.
- User Intent Evolution: Consistent throughout. Communication in Turkish expected.

2. Technical Foundation:
- Python 3.12.9 (system install): interpreter path `C:/Users/z0056bwr/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe` — MUST use this full path in terminal commands (not `python`).
- feedparser 6.0.12: RSS parsing. trafilatura 2.0.0: article extraction (JSON output with metadata). beautifulsoup4, requests, python-dotenv, lxml, tqdm available.
- Workspace root: `c:\Users\z0056bwr\Documents\phd\task first\new task\media-bias-pipeline`
- Round-robin balancing pattern across sources within each block to prevent single-outlet dominance.
- Project rules (CLAUDE.md): forward-only append logging to ai_usage/step_logs.md (one entry per step, written same turn), no config.yaml, settings from .env (.env not committed, .env.example is), prompts under prompts/, outlet_block is exploratory/descriptive metadata ONLY (never causal, never ground-truth ideology label), LLM labels are "silver/reference" not "gold".

3. Codebase Status:
- src/crawl.py (FULLY IMPLEMENTED, was a stub):
  - Purpose: Collect article URLs from RSS feeds, fetch+cache HTML, extract structured articles to data/processed/articles.jsonl
  - Current State: Complete and working. Imports: hashlib, json, os, time, pathlib, feedparser, requests, trafilatura, dotenv, and `from utils import PROCESSED, RAW_HTML, write_jsonl`.
  - RSS_FEEDS structure (nested dict block→source→list[feed_urls]):
    - left: theguardian (world/us-news/politics rss), npr (feeds.npr.org 1001/1014/1003), cnn (rss.cnn.com topstories/us/allpolitics)
    - right: foxnews (moxie.foxnews.com latest/politics/us/world/opinion), nypost (feed/news/us-news/politics), washingtonexaminer (feed/news/politics), washingtontimes (rss/headlines/news/ + politics), dailycaller (feed)
  - Constants: TARGET_PER_BLOCK=150, _DEFAULT_UA (browser Chrome UA string), USER_AGENT=os.getenv("USER_AGENT") or _DEFAULT_UA, CRAWL_DELAY_SEC=1.0, REQUEST_TIMEOUT=20, MIN_BODY_CHARS=400
  - Article schema: {article_id (sha1 of url), url, source, outlet_block, title, lead (description or first paragraph), body (trafilatura text), image_url, image_path (None), published_at (date), language ("en")}
  - Key functions: sha1(), collect_feed_urls() (dedup URLs, 1s delay per feed), fetch_html() (cache to RAW_HTML/<sha1>.html, 1 retry, NOTE: failures NOT cached so re-runs re-attempt them), extract_article() (trafilatura JSON, skip if body<400 chars), crawl_block() (round-robin), main()
- requirements.txt: Added `feedparser` line after `requests` in Core section.
- src/utils.py (unchanged): ROOT=parents[1], DATA, RAW_HTML=data/raw/html, PROCESSED=data/processed, ANALYSIS; read_jsonl(), write_jsonl(path, rows) returns count.
- data/processed/articles.jsonl: Written with 276 articles from second run; being overwritten by current third run.

- Issue: NPR article pages (www.npr.org) timed out at 30s each with default research UA, massively slowing crawl. Solution: Added browser-like Chrome User-Agent as default; verified NPR returns 200 in 0.7s with it.
- Issue: install_python_packages installed to wrong interpreter initially (ModuleNotFoundError). Solution: Ran `python.exe -m pip install` directly with full interpreter path.
- Issue: Right block only reached 126/150 (feeds exhausted: foxnews 66 + nypost 32 + washingtonexaminer 28 = 126 candidates). Solution: Added Fox world/opinion feeds, NYPost politics feed, and 2 new sources (washingtontimes ~40, dailycaller ~20) — all validated working.

edits.text:
"""One-off QA helper: print 5 sample articles per source from articles.jsonl."""
import json
from collections import defaultdict
from utils import PROCESSED

rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]
by_source = defaultdict(list)
for r in rows:
    by_source[r["source"]].append(r)

print(f"TOTAL articles: {len(rows)}")
print("By source:", {k: len(v) for k, v in by_source.items()})
print("By block:", {b: sum(1 for r in rows if r["outlet_block"] == b) for b in ("left", "right")})

for source in sorted(by_source):
    items = by_source[source]
    print("\n" + "=" * 90)
    print(f"SOURCE: {source}  (block={items[0]['outlet_block']}, n={len(items)})")
    print("=" * 90)
    for i, r in enumerate(items[:5], 1):
        body = (r.get("body") or "").replace("\n", " ")
        print(f"\n[{i}] title: {r.get('title')}")
        print(f"    url:        {r.get('url')}")
        print(f"    published:  {r.get('published_at')}")
        print(f"    image_url:  {r.get('image_url')}")
        print(f"    lead:       {(r.get('lead') or '')[:160]}")
        print(f"    body_len:   {len(r.get('body') or '')}")
        print(f"    body[:300]: {body[:300]}")

---

## Step 3 — Implement RSS crawler + collect 300 articles — 2026-06-01
- **Goal:** Build `src/crawl.py` (was a stub) and collect the planned 300 articles via RSS, balanced 150 left-block + 150 right-block, in the locked article schema. Boilerplate/ads in `body` are acceptable at this stage (cleaned in a later step).
- **Action:**
  - Implemented `src/crawl.py`: RSS URL collection (`feedparser`) → HTML fetch with on-disk cache (`data/raw/html/<sha1>.html`) → structured extraction (`trafilatura` JSON). Round-robin balancing across sources within each block so no single outlet dominates. `MIN_BODY_CHARS=400` skip threshold; `CRAWL_DELAY_SEC=1.0`; per-feed dedup by URL.
  - Article schema written to `data/processed/articles.jsonl`: `article_id` (sha1 of url), `url`, `source`, `outlet_block`, `title`, `lead`, `body`, `image_url`, `image_path` (None for now), `published_at`, `language`.
  - Added `feedparser` to `requirements.txt`.
  - **Outlet selection (exploratory `outlet_block` metadata only, NOT an ideology ground-truth):**
    - left: The Guardian (world/us-news/politics RSS), NPR (feeds.npr.org 1001/1014/1003), CNN (topstories/us/allpolitics RSS).
    - right: Fox News (latest/politics/us/world/opinion), New York Post (feed/news/us-news/politics), Washington Examiner (feed/news/politics), Washington Times (headlines/news + politics), Daily Caller (feed).
  - User-Agent: default to a browser-like Chrome UA (overridable via `USER_AGENT` in `.env`).
- **Prompt / model:** GitHub Copilot (Claude). Instruction (paraphrased): check whether RSS is set up, understand the system, implement crawling to fill left+right blocks with 300 articles, then verify 5 samples per outlet; ad/boilerplate text is fine for now.
- **Outcome — crawl run (2026-06-01):**
  - Wrote **300 articles** → `data/processed/articles.jsonl`.
  - By block: **left=150, right=150**.
  - By source: theguardian=70, npr=21, cnn=59, foxnews=34, nypost=34, washingtonexaminer=28, washingtontimes=34, dailycaller=20.
  - QA: inspected 5 samples per source; titles/leads/bodies/`image_url`/`published_at` populated and on-topic for all 8 outlets.
- **Problems / fixes:**
  - NPR article pages timed out (~30s each) under the original research UA → switched default to a browser-like Chrome UA; NPR then returned 200 quickly.
  - Right block initially reached only 126/150 (feeds exhausted) → expanded Fox (world/opinion) + NYPost (politics) feeds and added two sources (Washington Times, Daily Caller) to comfortably exceed 150 candidates.
- **Known issues (deferred to cleaning step):**
  - CNN `topstories` RSS contains stale 2023 `cnn-underscored` shopping links returning 403; they are skipped (not cached), so re-runs re-attempt ~14 junk URLs and slow the left block. Cosmetic only — left still reaches 150.
  - A few CNN live-news entries appear as near-duplicates (same story, different URL anchors); to be de-duplicated during cleaning.
- **Notes:** Failed fetches are intentionally not cached so transient failures are retried on re-run. Detailed sentence cleaning / dedup remains a separate upcoming step.

- **Blok dengesi: left=150, right=150** ✅ (hedefe tam ulaşıldı)

## 5'er örnek doğrulaması (8 outlet)
Her outlet'ten 5 örnek incelendi — `title`, `lead`, `body`, `image_url`, `published_at` alanları doluydu ve makaleler konuyla ilgiliydi. Ekstraksiyon kalitesi iyi.

## Önemli notlar (sonraki temizleme adımına bırakıldı)

import json
from collections import defaultdict
from utils import PROCESSED

rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]
by_source = defaultdict(list)
for r in rows:
    by_source[r["source"]].append(r)

print(f"TOTAL articles: {len(rows)}")
print("By source:", {k: len(v) for k, v in by_source.items()})
print("By block:", {b: sum(1 for r in rows if r["outlet_block"] == b) for b in ("left", "right")})

for source in sorted(by_source):
    items = by_source[source]
    print("\n" + "=" * 90)
    print(f"SOURCE: {source}  (block={items[0]['outlet_block']}, n={len(items)})")
    print("=" * 90)
    for i, r in enumerate(items[:5], 1):
        body = (r.get("body") or "").replace("\n", " ")
        print(f"\n[{i}] title: {r.get('title')}")
        print(f"    url:        {r.get('url')}")
        print(f"    published:  {r.get('published_at')}")
        print(f"    image_url:  {r.get('image_url')}")
        print(f"    lead:       {(r.get('lead') or '')[:160]}")
        print(f"    body_len:   {len(r.get('body') or '')}")
        print(f"    body[:300]: {body[:300]}")
"}

Append-only log. See [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the rule.
- **Goal:** Create the agreed repository skeleton, README, and forward-only AI-logging rule.

- **Action:** Created `media-bias-pipeline/` tree (src, data/{raw/html,processed,analysis/figures}, prompts, notebooks, ai_usage). Added placeholder files for all pipeline scripts, prompt templates, data outputs, `requirements.txt`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, compact `README.md`, and `CLAUDE.md` defining the logging rule.
- **Prompt / model:** GitHub Copilot (Claude). Instruction: read the task PDF, set up the given directory structure, keep README compact, encode the step-logs rule as a CLAUDE.md project rule.
- **Outcome:** Skeleton in place; no pipeline logic implemented yet. Files are placeholders/stubs ready for the next steps.
- **Notes:** Sub-task choice, proxy model selection, and crawl source still to decide in Step 2.
## Step 2 — Lock sub-task, models, collection plan; verify OpenAI model — 2026-06-01
- **Goal:** Commit the project to a concrete sub-task, proxy model, LLM/VLM model, and collection plan; encode the rules in code/docs.
- Sub-task fixed: **sentence-level subjective / opinionated framing bias** in English news articles.
- Collection plan fixed: **300 articles via RSS**, split **150 left-block + 150 right-block**; `outlet_block` flagged as exploratory metadata only.
- Proxy model fixed: **`GroNLP/mdebertav3-subjectivity-multilingual`** (OBJ/SUBJ); documented as a proxy, not a bias detector.

- LLM model fixed: **OpenAI `gpt-5.4-mini`**. **LLM annotation prompt design intentionally deferred**; `prompts/llm_sentence_annotation.txt` is now a placeholder describing the contract for the future prompt.

- VLM rule fixed: download → optional resize/compress → base64 inline; **no URLs sent to the VLM**; `image_url` stays in metadata only. Reflected in `prompts/vlm_image_annotation.txt` and `src/annotate_vlm.py`.
- Rewrote `README.md` (compact but covers sub-task, plan, units, models, outputs, analysis plan, claims-we-don't-make).
- Extended `CLAUDE.md` with Rules 4–6 (project conventions, external-model verification, VLM image handling).

- Rewrote `src/{crawl,sentence_split,proxy_label,annotate_llm,annotate_vlm,analyze}.py` as documented stubs that pin schemas, model IDs, and the two-phase cleaning split (minimal split now, detailed cleaning later as a separate logged step).
- **Prompt / model:** GitHub Copilot (Claude). Instruction: lock the above decisions, verify `gpt-5.4-mini` against official OpenAI docs before writing client code, and update repo accordingly.
- Modalities: text input/output + **image input** (vision supported); audio/video not supported.
- Endpoints include `v1/chat/completions` and `v1/responses`; structured outputs, function calling, streaming all supported.
- → Single model covers both LLM (text) and VLM (text+image) usage; no separate vision model needed.
- Concrete RSS feed list per outlet block is still to be picked in the crawl step; outlet selection will be logged there.
- Detailed sentence cleaning is deferred to its own step before LLM annotation.
- Sample size for LLM/VLM annotation will be locked when sampling logic lands (target 50–100 sentences for LLM; per-article main image for VLM).

Evet, çoğunluk güncel. Örnek tarihlere bakınca:

- **theguardian, npr, foxnews, nypost, washingtonexaminer, washingtontimes, dailycaller**: hepsinde tarihler **2026-05-29 → 2026-06-01** aralığında (yani son ~3 gün). ✅
- **CNN**: burada bir sorun var — CNN'in `topstories` RSS feed'i bayat (stale). Örneklerde gördüğümüz CNN makaleleri **2023-04-17/18** tarihli (Fox-Dominion davası haberleri). Yani 59 CNN makalesinin büyük kısmı muhtemelen 2023'ten.

Yani sol blokta 59 CNN makalesi eski. Bunu doğrulamak ve gerekirse düzeltmek ister misin? İki seçenek var:

1. **Hızlı doğrulama**: articles.jsonl'da kaynak başına tarih dağılımını çıkarıp net görelim.
2. **Düzeltme**: CNN'in bayat `topstories` feed'ini çıkar, sadece çalışır durumda güncel CNN RSS'i bul (CNN bazı feed'lerini kapattı — alternatif olarak `lite.cnn.com` veya başka bir merkezci-sol outlet, örn. Reuters/AP/Washington Post ile değiştirebiliriz). Sonra crawl'ı yeniden çalıştırırız.

toolCallRounds.response:
Evet, çoğunluk güncel. Örnek tarihlere bakınca:

- **theguardian, npr, foxnews, nypost, washingtonexaminer, washingtontimes, dailycaller**: hepsinde tarihler **2026-05-29 → 2026-06-01** aralığında (yani son ~3 gün). ✅
- **CNN**: burada bir sorun var — CNN'in `topstories` RSS feed'i bayat (stale). Örneklerde gördüğümüz CNN makaleleri **2023-04-17/18** tarihli (Fox-Dominion davası haberleri). Yani 59 CNN makalesinin büyük kısmı muhtemelen 2023'ten.

Yani sol blokta 59 CNN makalesi eski. Bunu doğrulamak ve gerekirse düzeltmek ister misin? İki seçenek var:

1. **Hızlı doğrulama**: articles.jsonl'da kaynak başına tarih dağılımını çıkarıp net görelim.
2. **Düzeltme**: CNN'in bayat `topstories` feed'ini çıkar, sadece çalışır durumda güncel CNN RSS'i bul (CNN bazı feed'lerini kapattı — alternatif olarak `lite.cnn.com` veya başka bir merkezci-sol outlet, örn. Reuters/AP/Washington Post ile değiştirebiliriz). Sonra crawl'ı yeniden çalıştırırız.

Hangisini yapayım?

thinking.text: Looking at the article dates in the samples, most are recent from 2026, but CNN's feed seems stale with older 2023 content mixed in, suggesting it might be pulling from an outdated source.

commandLine.original:
cd "C:\Users\z0056bwr\Documents\phd\task first\new task\media-bias-pipeline"; C:/Users/z0056bwr/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe -c "
import json, hashlib, os
from pathlib import Path
rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('data/processed/articles.jsonl', encoding='utf-8')]
cnn_urls=[r['url'] for r in rows if r['source']=='cnn']
print('cnn urls to evict:', len(cnn_urls))
raw=Path('data/raw/html')
removed=0
for u in cnn_urls:
    h=hashlib.sha1(u.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
    p=raw/f'{h}.html'
    if p.exists():
        p.unlink(); removed+=1
print('html files removed:', removed)
os.remove('data/processed/articles.jsonl')
print('articles.jsonl removed')
"

children.text: """Step: crawl — collect article URLs from RSS feeds, fetch HTML, and extract structured articles.
- Outlet block is exploratory metadata only, NOT a ground-truth ideology label.
"source":       str,              # outlet domain or short name
"image_url":    str | None,       # main image URL (metadata only; never sent to VLM)
"image_path":   str | None,       # local cached path once downloaded
- Body text is intentionally NOT heavily cleaned here. Ads / boilerplate / promo
sentences may remain; detailed sentence cleaning is a separate later step.
- `outlet_block` is descriptive metadata only, not a ground-truth ideology label
from utils import PROCESSED, RAW_HTML, write_jsonl
# Each block maps a short source name -> list of RSS feed URLs for that outlet.
# `outlet_block` is exploratory metadata only (descriptive, never causal, never a
# ground-truth ideology label). Outlets are widely categorised by independent media
# bias raters; we use that only to balance the two collection buckets.
# Some outlets (e.g. npr.org) hang/stall on non-browser User-Agents, so default to a
# realistic browser UA. Can still be overridden via .env.

import json, hashlib, os
from pathlib import Path
rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('data/processed/articles.jsonl', encoding='utf-8')]
cnn_urls=[r['url'] for r in rows if r['source']=='cnn']
print('cnn urls to evict:', len(cnn_urls))
raw=Path('data/raw/html')
removed=0
for u in cnn_urls:
    h=hashlib.sha1(u.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
    p=raw/f'{h}.html'
    if p.exists():
        p.unlink(); removed+=1
print('html files removed:', removed)
os.remove('data/processed/articles.jsonl')
print('articles.jsonl removed')
"

cd "C:\Users\z0056bwr\Documents\phd\task first\new task\media-bias-pipeline"; C:/Users/z0056bwr/AppData/Local/Programs/Python/Python312/python.exe -c "
import json
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('data/processed/articles.jsonl', encoding='utf-8')]
by_src=defaultdict(list)
for r in rows: by_src[r['source']].append((r.get('published_at') or '')[:7])
for s,v in sorted(by_src.items()):
    print(f'{s:22s} n={len(v):3d}  {dict(Counter(v).most_common())}')
print()
print('--- HuffPost samples ---')
hp=[r for r in rows if r['source']=='huffpost'][:5]
for i,r in enumerate(hp,1):
    body=(r.get('body') or '').replace(chr(10),' ')
    print(f'[{i}] {r.get(\"published_at\")} | {r.get(\"title\")}')
    print(f'    url: {r.get(\"url\")}')
    print(f'    lead: {(r.get(\"lead\") or \"\")[:140]}')
    print(f'    body_len={len(r.get(\"body\") or \"\")}  body[:200]: {body[:200]}')
"

edits.text:
"""One-off QA helper: date distribution + HuffPost samples."""
import json
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
from utils import PROCESSED

rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]
by_src = defaultdict(list)
for r in rows:
    by_src[r["source"]].append((r.get("published_at") or "")[:7])

print(f"TOTAL: {len(rows)}")
print("By block:", {b: sum(1 for r in rows if r['outlet_block'] == b) for b in ('left', 'right')})
print()
for s, v in sorted(by_src.items()):
    print(f"{s:22s} n={len(v):3d}  {dict(Counter(v).most_common())}")

print("\n--- HuffPost samples ---")
hp = [r for r in rows if r["source"] == "huffpost"][:5]
for i, r in enumerate(hp, 1):
    body = (r.get("body") or "").replace("\n", " ")
    print(f"\n[{i}] {r.get('published_at')} | {r.get('title')}")
    print(f"    url:       {r.get('url')}")
    print(f"    image_url: {r.get('image_url')}")
    print(f"    lead:      {(r.get('lead') or '')[:160]}")
    print(f"    body_len={len(r.get('body') or '')}  body[:240]: {body[:240]}")

---

## Step 3b — Replace stale CNN feeds with HuffPost; re-crawl — 2026-06-01
- **Goal:** Ensure all 300 articles are *current* (last few days). QA on Step 3 output revealed CNN's `rss.cnn.com/*` feeds are stale: 58/59 CNN articles dated 2023-04, 1/59 dated 2023-03. All other 7 outlets were fresh (2026-05/06).
- **Action:**
  - Removed CNN from `RSS_FEEDS['left']` in `src/crawl.py` (with a code comment explaining why).
  - Added **HuffPost** as the replacement left-block outlet, with three working feeds: `chaski.huffpost.com/us/auto/vertical/{politics,us-news,world-news}` (validated: 74 fresh candidate URLs, top entry from today).
  - Evicted the 59 cached CNN HTML files from `data/raw/html/` and deleted the old `data/processed/articles.jsonl`, then re-ran `crawl.py`.
- **Outcome — re-crawl (2026-06-01):**
  - Wrote **300 articles**; **left=150, right=150**.
  - By source: theguardian=64, huffpost=63, npr=23, foxnews=35, nypost=35, washingtontimes=34, washingtonexaminer=26, dailycaller=20.
  - Date freshness: every outlet now dominated by **2026-05 / 2026-06** entries (only 1 washingtonexaminer article from 2026-04). No 2023 leakage.
  - QA: inspected 5 HuffPost samples — titles/leads/bodies/`image_url`/`published_at` all populated and on-topic.
- **Notes:** HuffPost is, like the original CNN choice, widely categorised as left-of-centre by independent media bias raters; this is still exploratory `outlet_block` metadata only and remains NOT a ground-truth ideology label.

CNN'i çıkardım (rss.cnn.com feed'leri kullanılamaz durumda), yerine **HuffPost** ekledim (sol-eğilimli, çalışan güncel feed'ler: politics + us-news + world-news).

## Yeni durum — 300 article, hepsi güncel
- **Blok:** left=150, right=150 ✅
- **Kaynak:** theguardian=64, huffpost=63, npr=23, foxnews=35, nypost=35, washingtontimes=34, washingtonexaminer=26, dailycaller=20

- HuffPost örneklerinde başlık/lead/body/image_url/published_at hepsi dolu ve güncel (Trump, MAGA, Iran, Krugman vb. günlük politika konuları).

- src/crawl.py: CNN bloğu HuffPost ile değiştirildi (kod yorumunda neden açıklandı)

edits.text:
"""One-off QA: distinct check + URL-derived topic distribution by block."""
import json
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from utils import PROCESSED

rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]

# 1) Distinct checks
ids = [r["article_id"] for r in rows]
urls = [r["url"] for r in rows]
titles_norm = [re.sub(r"\s+", " ", (r.get("title") or "")).strip().lower() for r in rows]

print(f"TOTAL rows:           {len(rows)}")
print(f"distinct article_id:  {len(set(ids))}")
print(f"distinct url:         {len(set(urls))}")
print(f"distinct title (norm):{len(set(titles_norm))}")
dup_titles = [t for t, c in Counter(titles_norm).items() if c > 1 and t]
print(f"duplicate-title groups (n>1): {len(dup_titles)}")
for t in dup_titles[:10]:
    print(f"  - x{Counter(titles_norm)[t]}  {t[:110]}")

# 2) Topic heuristic from URL path
TOPIC_KEYWORDS = {
    "politics":   ["politic", "election", "congress", "senate", "white-house", "gop", "democrat"],
    "us":         ["us-news", "/us/", "national", "/section/news/"],
    "world":      ["world", "international", "global", "foreign"],
    "opinion":    ["opinion", "op-ed", "commentary", "editorial", "/voices/"],
    "business":   ["business", "econom", "money", "market", "finance"],
    "tech":       ["tech", "science", "ai-", "/ai/", "innovation"],
    "health":     ["health", "covid", "medic", "wellness"],
    "entertainment":["entertain", "celebrit", "tv-", "movie", "music", "media"],
    "sports":     ["sport", "nfl", "nba", "mlb", "soccer", "olymp"],
    "lifestyle":  ["lifestyle", "food", "travel", "home", "fashion"],
}

def topic_of(url: str) -> str:
    p = urlparse(url).path.lower()
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_KEYWORDS.items():
        if any(kw in p for kw in kws):
            return topic
    return "other"

by_block_topic = defaultdict(Counter)
by_topic_total = Counter()
for r in rows:
    t = topic_of(r["url"])
    by_block_topic[r["outlet_block"]][t] += 1
    by_topic_total[t] += 1

print("\n=== Topic distribution by block (URL-heuristic) ===")
topics = sorted(by_topic_total, key=lambda x: -by_topic_total[x])
print(f"{'topic':15s} {'left':>6s} {'right':>6s} {'total':>6s}")
for t in topics:
    print(f"{t:15s} {by_block_topic['left'][t]:>6d} {by_block_topic['right'][t]:>6d} {by_topic_total[t]:>6d}")
print(f"{'TOTAL':15s} {sum(by_block_topic['left'].values()):>6d} {sum(by_block_topic['right'].values()):>6d} {sum(by_topic_total.values()):>6d}")

# 3) Per-source topic breakdown (compact)
print("\n=== Topic distribution by source ===")
by_src_topic = defaultdict(Counter)
for r in rows:
    by_src_topic[r["source"]][topic_of(r["url"])] += 1
for src in sorted(by_src_topic):
    items = ", ".join(f"{t}={n}" for t, n in by_src_topic[src].most_common())
    print(f"  {src:22s} {items}")

edits.text:
---

## Step 3c — Distinct check + topic distribution QA — 2026-06-01
- **Goal:** Confirm the 300-article corpus has no duplicates and inspect topic spread across blocks.
- **Action:** Ran a QA script over `data/processed/articles.jsonl` to (a) count distinct `article_id`, `url`, and normalised title; (b) bucket articles into topics using a URL-path keyword heuristic and cross-tab by `outlet_block` and source.
- **Outcome — distinct:** 300 / 300 unique on `article_id`, `url`, and normalised title. **No duplicates.**
- **Outcome — topic distribution by block (URL heuristic):**
  - politics: left=27, right=37 (total 64)
  - us: left=10, right=16 (26)
  - world: left=22, right=3 (25)
  - sports: left=1, right=11 (12)
  - opinion: left=0, right=7 (7)
  - business: left=4, right=3 (7)
  - entertainment: left=0, right=6 (6)
  - health: left=2, right=4 (6)
  - tech: left=3, right=3 (6)
  - lifestyle: left=1, right=3 (4)
  - other (unmatched URL paths): left=80, right=57 (137)
  - TOTAL: left=150, right=150, total=300
- **Caveats:**
  - The "other" bucket is large (137/300) because some outlets (notably HuffPost `/entry/<slug>`, washingtontimes, dailycaller) bury the topic outside the URL path. The heuristic is a sanity check, **not a ground-truth topic label**. A proper topic field can be added later from feed-name or a lightweight classifier if needed.
  - Right block skews to us-news/politics/opinion; left block skews to world (Guardian-driven). This is descriptive only; the project does not interpret topic mix as evidence of bias.

edits.text:
"""Diagnostic: per-outlet URL path patterns to check topic-from-URL feasibility."""
import json
from collections import Counter
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from utils import PROCESSED

rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]

by_src = {}
for r in rows:
    by_src.setdefault(r["source"], []).append(r["url"])

for src in sorted(by_src):
    urls = by_src[src]
    # first 2 path segments
    segs = []
    for u in urls:
        p = [s for s in urlparse(u).path.split("/") if s]
        segs.append("/".join(p[:2]) if len(p) >= 2 else "/".join(p))
    c = Counter(segs)
    print(f"\n=== {src}  n={len(urls)} ===")
    for k, v in c.most_common():
        print(f"  {v:3d}  /{k}/")
    # also: first segment only
    first = Counter([s.split("/")[0] for s in segs])
    print(f"  -- first-segment buckets: {dict(first.most_common())}")

response.value:
Looking at how different news outlets structure their URLs, I can see that some include topic categories in the path while others use generic slug patterns. Fox News has a clear topic structure with sections like politics and sports, but Daily Caller and HuffPost bury their content under generic URL patterns that don't reveal the topic.

I'm realizing my initial analysis of NYPost was incomplete — their URLs actually do contain section information like `/us-news/` or `/opinion/`, just positioned after the date rather than at the beginning. Washington Times similarly has `/news/` as a prefix but lacks more granular topic categorization. So the outlets that make topics readily visible in their URLs are Fox News, The Guardian, and Washington Examiner, while the others require parsing the slug or other metadata to determine content category.

The user wants me to add a feed-origin field to track which RSS feed each article came from, which will help derive topics more reliably. My approach is to modify the crawler to record the feed URL alongside each article, then use that feed name (like `politics.xml`) as the primary source for topic assignment, falling back to URL path analysis when needed. After re-crawling with this new tracking, I can categorize articles into political versus non-political buckets and show the distribution across sources.

Modifying the crawler now...

Tanı sonucu net:

| outlet | URL'de konu var mı? |
|---|---|
| foxnews, theguardian, washingtonexaminer | ✅ var (`/politics/`, `/world/`, `/opinion/`, `/sports/` vb.) |
| nypost | ✅ var (4. segmentte: `/2026/06/01/us-news/...`) |
| npr, huffpost, washingtontimes, dailycaller | ❌ yok (sadece tarih+slug) |

Yani 4 outlet için **mutlaka feed-of-origin** lazım. Senin önerinle gidiyorum:
1.
'a

ekle, yeniden çek (cache sayesinde hızlı).
2. `topic`'i hibrid türet: önce URL segmenti, sonra feed adı.
3. Politics > us > world > opinion sonra non-political dağılım göster.
Şimdi
ekleyip schema'yı genişletiyorum.

edits.text: "feed_url": feed_url,
, feed_url: str

tuple[str, str]]:
    """Return de-duplicated (article_url, feed_url) pairs in feed-read order.

    `feed_url` is the RSS feed the article URL was *first* seen in. We keep this so
    downstream steps can derive a publisher-provided topic label (e.g. .../politics.xml).
    """
    seen: set[str] = set()
    pairs: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    for feed_url in feeds:
        try:
            resp = session.get(feed_url, timeout=REQUEST_TIMEOUT)
            parsed = feedparser.parse(resp.content)
        except Exception as exc:  # network / parse error — skip this feed
            print(f"    ! feed error {feed_url}: {exc}")
            continue
        n_before = len(pairs)
        for entry in parsed.entries:
            link = entry.get("link")
            if not link:
                continue
            link = link.split("#")[0].strip()
            if link in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(link)
            pairs.append((link, feed_url))
        print(f"    {feed_url} -> {len(pairs) - n_before} new urls")
        time.sleep(CRAWL_DELAY_SEC)
    return pair

,
          "feed_url":     str               # RSS feed the URL was first seen in (topic signal)

edits.text:
"""Assign a topic to each article using a hybrid rule:
   1) URL path segments (where outlets put the section in the URL).
   2) Fall back to the originating feed_url (where the URL is just date/slug).

Then bucket topics into: politics-related (politics > us > world > opinion) vs
non-political (sports, entertainment, business, lifestyle, health, tech, other).
"""
import json
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from utils import PROCESSED

# Canonical topic keywords. Order matters: politics first, then us, world, opinion,
# then non-political buckets.
TOPIC_RULES = [
    ("politics",      ["politic", "election", "congress", "senate", "white-house",
                       "campaigns", "gop", "democrat", "1014"]),  # 1014 = NPR Politics
    ("us",            ["us-news", "us_news", "/us/", "national", "1003",
                       "/section/news/feed"]),  # 1003 = NPR National
    ("world",         ["/world", "world-news", "world.xml", "international",
                       "foreign", "australia-news", "uk-news"]),
    ("opinion",       ["opinion", "op-ed", "commentisfree", "/voices/", "editorial",
                       "columnist"]),
    ("business",      ["business", "/money", "market", "finance", "econom"]),
    ("tech",          ["/tech", "/science", "/ai-", "/ai/"]),
    ("health",        ["/health", "covid", "medic", "wellness", "environment"]),
    ("entertainment", ["entertain", "celebrit", "tv-", "movie", "music", "media",
                       "magazine", "obituary"]),
    ("sports",        ["/sport", "outkick", "nfl", "nba", "mlb", "soccer", "olymp"]),
    ("lifestyle",     ["lifestyle", "/food", "/travel", "/home", "fashion",
                       "in_focus"]),
    ("us-general",    ["1001", "front-page", "moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/latest",
                       "nypost.com/feed", "washingtonexaminer.com/feed",
                       "washingtontimes.com/rss/headlines/news/",
                       "dailycaller.com/feed"]),  # generic "news" feeds → unspecified
]
POLITICS_RELATED = {"politics", "us", "world", "opinion", "us-general"}


def topic_of(url: str, feed_url: str) -> str:
    """First match wins, scanning rules in order. URL path takes precedence; feed
    is the fallback for outlets whose URLs don't expose a section."""
    path = urlparse(url).path.lower()
    feed = (feed_url or "").lower()
    # 1) URL path
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_RULES:
        if any(kw in path for kw in kws):
            return topic
    # 2) Feed URL
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_RULES:
        if any(kw in feed for kw in kws):
            return topic
    return "other"


rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]

# 1) Coverage per source: how many "other" / unlabelled?
print(f"TOTAL: {len(rows)}")
by_src_topic = defaultdict(Counter)
for r in rows:
    t = topic_of(r["url"], r.get("feed_url", ""))
    by_src_topic[r["source"]][t] += 1

print("\n=== Per-source topic coverage (any 'other'?) ===")
for src in sorted(by_src_topic):
    c = by_src_topic[src]
    n = sum(c.values())
    other = c.get("other", 0)
    flag = "  <-- UNCOVERED" if other > 0 else ""
    print(f"  {src:22s} n={n:3d}  other={other}{flag}")
    print(f"      {dict(c.most_common())}")

# 2) Topic × block crosstab, politics-related first
by_block_topic = defaultdict(Counter)
for r in rows:
    by_block_topic[r["outlet_block"]][topic_of(r["url"], r.get("feed_url", ""))] += 1

print("\n=== Topic × block (politics-related first) ===")
ordered_topics = [t for t, _ in TOPIC_RULES] + ["other"]
seen = set()
ordered_topics = [t for t in ordered_topics if not (t in seen or seen.add(t))]
totals = Counter()
for t in ordered_topics:
    totals[t] = by_block_topic["left"][t] + by_block_topic["right"][t]
print(f"{'topic':15s} {'group':18s} {'left':>5s} {'right':>5s} {'total':>6s}")
for t in ordered_topics:
    if totals[t] == 0:
        continue
    grp = "politics-related" if t in POLITICS_RELATED else "non-political"
    print(f"{t:15s} {grp:18s} {by_block_topic['left'][t]:>5d} {by_block_topic['right'][t]:>5d} {totals[t]:>6d}")

# 3) Bucketed
pol_left = sum(by_block_topic["left"][t] for t in POLITICS_RELATED)
pol_right = sum(by_block_topic["right"][t] for t in POLITICS_RELATED)
nonpol_left = 150 - pol_left
nonpol_right = 150 - pol_right
print()
print(f"POLITICS-RELATED:  left={pol_left}  right={pol_right}  total={pol_left+pol_right}")
print(f"NON-POLITICAL:     left={nonpol_left}  right={nonpol_right}  total={nonpol_left+nonpol_right}")

response.value:
All 300 articles are now categorized with no "other" bucket, and every outlet is covered. The "us-general" category is tricky though — it captures articles from generic news feeds across outlets like NPR, Fox News, and others where there's no specific topic indicator, so I've been labeling them as political content by default.

But that's not quite honest. These are really uncategorized articles that we can't specifically classify, so I should present them separately rather than forcing them into politics. Looking at the actual distribution, I'm thinking of reorganizing into three cleaner groups: politics-related content (politics, us, world, opinion combined), non-political topics (sports, entertainment, business, tech, health, lifestyle), and general news as its own uncategorized bucket. Let me adjust the script to reflect this more accurate grouping.

Tüm 8 outlet için **other=0** — her makaleye topic atandı. Sonuçlar:

## Per-source kapsama (0 "other")
Hepsi başarılı. Özellikle:
- **huffpost** (URL'de hiç ipucu yok): 42 politics + 19 us + 1 sports + 1 entertainment → feed adından temizce çözüldü
- **npr** (URL'de yok): 7 politics + 6 us + 10 us-general (genel News feed)
- **washingtontimes / dailycaller**: feed adından bölündü

## Topic × Blok dağılımı

| topic | grup | left | right | total |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **politics** | politics | 62 | 48 | **110** |
| **us** | politics | 33 | 18 | **51** |
| **world** | politics | 29 | 2 | **31** |
| **opinion** | politics | 8 | 6 | **14** |
| us-general* | belirsiz | 10 | 45 | **55** |
| business | non-pol | 4 | 3 | 7 |
| sports | non-pol | 1 | 14 | 15 |
| entertainment | non-pol | 1 | 8 | 9 |
| tech | non-pol | 0 | 3 | 3 |
| lifestyle | non-pol | 0 | 3 | 3 |
| health | non-pol | 2 | 0 | 2 |

\* `us-general` = "genel News" feed'inden geldi (örn. nypost `/feed/`, examiner `/feed`, washingtontimes `/news/`, dailycaller `/feed`). Konu açıkça belirtilmemiş; çoğu muhtemelen politika ama emin değiliz.

## Bucket özeti (us-general'ı ayrı tutarak)
- **Saf politika ilişkili** (politics + us + world + opinion): **left=132, right=74, total=206**
- **Genel haber (sınıflanmamış)**: left=10, right=45, total=55
- **Non-political** (sports/entertainment/business/tech/health/lifestyle): left=8, right=31, total=39

## Gözlemler
- Left bloğu (özellikle Guardian + HuffPost) ezici biçimde politika/dünya haberleri
- Right bloğu daha çeşitli: spor (15, çoğu Fox + NYPost), entertainment (8), us-general (45) yüksek
- Konu kapsama sorunu yok —

ekleme adımı işe yaradı

## Değişiklikler
- src/crawl.py:

alanı article schema'sına eklendi (collect_feed_urls artık (url, feed_url) çiftleri döndürüyor)
- data/processed/articles.jsonl: yeniden yazıldı, her makalede

var
- Topic atama mantığı src/_assign_topics.py içinde validate edildi

## Sorum / sonraki adım
Topic'i kalıcı tutmak ister misin? İki seçenek:
1. **Topic'i ayrıca saklama** (önerilen değil): yukarıdaki gibi gerektiğinde `_assign_topics.py` mantığıyla türet — schema temiz kalır
2. **Topic alanını articles.jsonl'a eklemek**: tek bir betikle her makaleye `topic` alanı yazılır, kalıcı olur

Ve "us-general" bucket'ını politics-related sayalım mı, yoksa "uncategorized" olarak ayrı mı tutalım? (Önerim: ayrı tut, çünkü içeriği bilmiyoruz.)

{"content": """"Diagnostic: per-outlet URL path patterns to check topic-from-URL feasibility."""
import json
from collections import Counter
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from utils import PROCESSED

rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]

by_src = {}
for r in rows:
    by_src.setdefault(r["source"], []).append(r["url"])

for src in sorted(by_src):
    urls = by_src[src]
    # first 2 path segments
    segs = []
    for u in urls:
        p = [s for s in urlparse(u).path.split("/") if s]
        segs.append("/".join(p[:2]) if len(p) >= 2 else "/".join(p))
    c = Counter(segs)
    print(f"\n=== {src}  n={len(urls)} ===")
    for k, v in c.most_common():
        print(f"  {v:3d}  /{k}/")
    # also: first segment only
    first = Counter([s.split("/")[0] for s in segs])
    print(f"  -- first-segment buckets: {dict(first.most_common())}")

Washington Times similarly has `/news/` as a prefix but lacks more granular topic categorization. So the outlets that make topics readily visible in their URLs are Fox News, The Guardian, and Washington Examiner, while the others require parsing the slug or other metadata to determine content category.

The user wants me to add a feed-origin field to track which RSS feed each article came from, which will help derive topics more reliably. My approach is to modify the crawler to record the feed URL alongside each article, then use that feed name (like `politics.xml`) as the primary source for topic assignment, falling back to URL path analysis when needed. After re-crawling with this new tracking, I can categorize articles into political versus non-political buckets and show the distribution across sources.

toolCalls.name: create_file
toolCalls.arguments:
{"content": """"Assign a topic to each article using a hybrid rule:
   1) URL path segments (where outlets put the section in the URL).
   2) Fall back to the originating feed_url (where the URL is just date/slug).

Then bucket topics into: politics-related (politics > us > world > opinion) vs
non-political (sports, entertainment, business, lifestyle, health, tech, other).
"""
import json
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from utils import PROCESSED

# Canonical topic keywords. Order matters: politics first, then us, world, opinion,
# then non-political buckets.
TOPIC_RULES = [
    ("politics",      ["politic", "election", "congress", "senate", "white-house",
                       "campaigns", "gop", "democrat", "1014"]),  # 1014 = NPR Politics
    ("us",            ["us-news", "us_news", "/us/", "national", "1003",
                       "/section/news/feed"]),  # 1003 = NPR National
    ("world",         ["/world", "world-news", "world.xml", "international",
                       "foreign", "australia-news", "uk-news"]),
    ("opinion",       ["opinion", "op-ed", "commentisfree", "/voices/", "editorial",
                       "columnist"]),
    ("business",      ["business", "/money", "market", "finance", "econom"]),
    ("tech",          ["/tech", "/science", "/ai-", "/ai/"]),
    ("health",        ["/health", "covid", "medic", "wellness", "environment"]),
    ("entertainment", ["entertain", "celebrit", "tv-", "movie", "music", "media",
                       "magazine", "obituary"]),
    ("sports",        ["/sport", "outkick", "nfl", "nba", "mlb", "soccer", "olymp"]),
    ("lifestyle",     ["lifestyle", "/food", "/travel", "/home", "fashion",
                       "in_focus"]),
    ("us-general",    ["1001", "front-page", "moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/latest",
                       "nypost.com/feed", "washingtonexaminer.com/feed",
                       "washingtontimes.com/rss/headlines/news/",
                       "dailycaller.com/feed"]),  # generic "news" feeds → unspecified
]
POLITICS_RELATED = {"politics", "us", "world", "opinion", "us-general"}


def topic_of(url: str, feed_url: str) -> str:
    """First match wins, scanning rules in order. URL path takes precedence; feed
    is the fallback for outlets whose URLs don't expose a section."""
    path = urlparse(url).path.lower()
    feed = (feed_url or "").lower()
    # 1) URL path
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_RULES:
        if any(kw in path for kw in kws):
            return topic
    # 2) Feed URL
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_RULES:
        if any(kw in feed for kw in kws):
            return topic
    return "other"


rows = [json.loads(l) for l in open(PROCESSED / "articles.jsonl", encoding="utf-8")]

# 1) Coverage per source: how many "other" / unlabelled?
print(f"TOTAL: {len(rows)}")
by_src_topic = defaultdict(Counter)
for r in rows:
    t = topic_of(r["url"], r.get("feed_url", ""))
    by_src_topic[r["source"]][t] += 1

print("\n=== Per-source topic coverage (any 'other'?) ===")
for src in sorted(by_src_topic):
    c = by_src_topic[src]
    n = sum(c.values())
    other = c.get("other", 0)
    flag = "  <-- UNCOVERED" if other > 0 else ""
    print(f"  {src:22s} n={n:3d}  other={other}{flag}")
    print(f"      {dict(c.most_common())}")

# 2) Topic × block crosstab, politics-related first
by_block_topic = defaultdict(Counter)
for r in rows:
    by_block_topic[r["outlet_block"]][topic_of(r["url"], r.get("feed_url", ""))] += 1

print("\n=== Topic × block (politics-related first) ===")
ordered_topics = [t for t, _ in TOPIC_RULES] + ["other"]
seen = set()
ordered_topics = [t for t in ordered_topics if not (t in seen or seen.add(t))]
totals = Counter()
for t in ordered_topics:
    totals[t] = by_block_topic["left"][t] + by_block_topic["right"][t]
print(f"{'topic':15s} {'group':18s} {'left':>5s} {'right':>5s} {'total':>6s}")
for t in ordered_topics:
    if totals[t] == 0:
        continue
    grp = "politics-related" if t in POLITICS_RELATED else "non-political"
    print(f"{t:15s} {grp:18s} {by_block_topic['left'][t]:>5d} {by_block_topic['right'][t]:>5d} {totals[t]:>6d}")

# 3) Bucketed
pol_left = sum(by_block_topic["left"][t] for t in POLITICS_RELATED)
pol_right = sum(by_block_topic["right"][t] for t in POLITICS_RELATED)
nonpol_left = 150 - pol_left
nonpol_right = 150 - pol_right
print()
print(f"POLITICS-RELATED:  left={pol_left}  right={pol_right}  total={pol_left+pol_right}")
print(f"NON-POLITICAL:     left={nonpol_left}  right={nonpol_right}  total={nonpol_left+nonpol_right}")

All 300 articles are now categorized with no "other" bucket, and every outlet is covered. The "us-general" category is tricky though — it captures articles from generic news feeds across outlets like NPR, Fox News, and others where there's no specific topic indicator, so I've been labeling them as political content by default.

But that's not quite honest. These are really uncategorized articles that we can't specifically classify, so I should present them separately rather than forcing them into politics. Looking at the actual distribution, I'm thinking of reorganizing into three cleaner groups: politics-related content (politics, us, world, opinion combined), non-political topics (sports, entertainment, business, tech, health, lifestyle), and general news as its own uncategorized bucket. Let me adjust the script to reflect this more accurate grouping.

        "article_id": sha1(url),
        "url": url,

# --- Topic taxonomy ---------------------------------------------------------
# First-match-wins keyword rules, scanned in order against the article URL path
# first, then the originating feed URL. Topics group into three buckets via
# TOPIC_GROUP. "general" is used when only a generic "news" feed is available
# (e.g. nypost /feed/, npr 1001 News, washingtontimes /news/, dailycaller /feed/);
# we DO NOT silently fold these into "political" because their actual subject is
# unverified.
TOPIC_RULES: list[tuple[str, list[str]]] = [
    ("politics",      ["politic", "election", "congress", "senate", "white-house",
                       "campaigns", "gop", "democrat", "1014"]),  # NPR 1014 = Politics
    ("us",            ["us-news", "us_news", "/us/", "national", "1003",
                       "/section/news/feed"]),                   # NPR 1003 = National
    ("world",         ["/world", "world-news", "world.xml", "international",
                       "foreign", "australia-news", "uk-news"]),
    ("opinion",       ["opinion", "op-ed", "commentisfree", "/voices/", "editorial",
                       "columnist"]),
    ("business",      ["business", "/money", "market", "finance", "econom"]),
    ("tech",          ["/tech", "/science", "/ai-", "/ai/"]),
    ("health",        ["/health", "covid", "medic", "wellness", "environment"]),
    ("entertainment", ["entertain", "celebrit", "tv-", "movie", "music", "media",
                       "magazine", "obituary"]),
    ("sports",        ["/sport", "outkick", "nfl", "nba", "mlb", "soccer", "olymp"]),
    ("lifestyle",     ["lifestyle", "/food", "/travel", "/home", "fashion",
                       "in_focus"]),
    ("general",       ["1001", "front-page", "moxie.foxnews.com/google-publisher/latest",
                       "nypost.com/feed", "washingtonexaminer.com/feed",
                       "washingtontimes.com/rss/headlines/news/",
                       "dailycaller.com/feed"]),
]
TOPIC_GROUP: dict[str, str] = {
    "politics": "political", "us": "political", "world": "political", "opinion": "political",
    "business": "non-political", "tech": "non-political", "health": "non-political",
    "entertainment": "non-political", "sports": "non-political", "lifestyle": "non-political",
    "general": "uncategorized",
}


def assign_topic(url: str, feed_url: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Return (topic, topic_group). URL path wins over feed URL."""
    from urllib.parse import urlparse
    path = urlparse(url).path.lower()
    feed = (feed_url or "").lower()
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_RULES:
        if any(kw in path for kw in kws):
            return topic, TOPIC_GROUP[topic]
    for topic, kws in TOPIC_RULES:
        if any(kw in feed for kw in kws):
            return topic, TOPIC_GROUP[topic]
    return "general", TOPIC_GROUP["general"]

,              # RSS feed the URL was first seen in (topic signal)
          "topic":        str,              # one of TOPIC_RULES keys, or "general"
          "topic_group":  "political" | "non-political" | "uncategorized"
